mission.title: woodpecker-ci: Allow notification plugins to notify about the pipeline status
mission.description: Allow notification plugins to notify about the pipeline status. This issue affects 9+ users.

steps.title: Identify woodpecker-ci troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your woodpecker-ci deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci -n woodpecker-ci --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review woodpecker-ci configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant woodpecker-ci configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci
kubectl get configmap -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=woodpecker-ci
```
### Clear and concise description of the problem

The env var `CI_PIPELINE_STATUS` war broken already and has always reported `success` but was now removed completely in https://github.com/woodpecker-ci/woodpecker/pull/3846

Right now, I don't see

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow notification plugins to notify about the pipeline…
steps.description:
remove some environment variables who are either the same value or make no sense to exist in the first place:

CI_STEP_STATUS
CI_STEP_FINISHED
CI_PIPELINE_STATUS
CI_PIPELINE_FINISHED
CI_COMMIT_URL
```yaml
steps:
   - name: slack
     image: plugins/slack
     settings:
       channel: dev
     when:
       - status: [ success, failure ]
```

steps.title: Confirm Allow notification plugins to notify about the… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci -n woodpecker-ci --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n woodpecker-ci --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
remove some environment variables who are either the same value or make no sense to exist in the first place:

CI_STEP_STATUS
CI_STEP_FINISHED
CI_PIPELINE_STATUS
CI_PIPELINE_FINISHED
CI_COMMIT_URL
codeSnippets:
steps:
   - name: slack
     image: plugins/slack
     settings:
       channel: dev
     when:
       - status: [ success, failure ]

steps:
   - name: notify
     image: debian:stable-slim
     commands:
       - echo notifying
runs_on: [ success, failure ]

steps:
   - name: slack
     image: plugins/slack
     settings:
       channel: dev
     when:
       - status: [ success, failure ]

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with woodpecker-ci installed or the issue environment reproducible.
